name: BigTreeTech SKR Pico
marlin_ref_note: RP2040 boards (SKR Pico) need Marlin's bugfix-2.1.x branch — RP2040 support isn't in the latest stable release, it lands in Marlin 2.1.3.
serial_port_note: -1 = native USB CDC, so AnglerOS (USB-OTG) and desktop hosts like OctoPrint/Pronterface can talk to the board over its USB port.
serial_port_2_note: UART0 = the SKR Pico's TFT header, giving the T-Dongle-S3 QWIIC UART its own port alongside USB.
flash_hint: Hold BOOT on the SKR Pico and plug in USB (it appears as a drive named RPI-RP2), then copy the firmware onto that drive. The board reboots into Marlin.
notes: TMC2209 drivers share one UART with per-driver addressing; the SKR Pico pins file handles that automatically once MOTHERBOARD is set.
